{"step_by_step_thinking": "Locally advanced breast cancer is defined as cancer that has grown beyond the breast and nearby lymph nodes. Option A (Tumour more than 4cm) is a common criterion for locally advanced breast cancer. Option B (Inflammatory breast cancer) is a type of breast cancer that often presents with skin involvement and is considered locally advanced. Option C (Chest wall involvement) indicates that the cancer has spread to the chest wall, which is a sign of locally advanced breast cancer. Option D (Skin involvement) alone does not necessarily indicate locally advanced breast cancer, as it can be seen in early stages of the disease. Therefore, the option that does not classify as locally advanced breast cancer is D.", "answer_choice": "D"}
